proof_boundary.allowed_use: Internal and buyer-prep procurement readiness map for a guided pilot.
not_proof_of: SOC 2, ISO/IEC 42001, ISO/IEC 27001, FedRAMP, or formal security certification
buyer approval or willingness to pay
proof_boundary.buyer_safe_positioning: Security and AI-governance proof path is explicit, but enterprise security proof remains pending owner approvals and hosted/testing evidence.

current_source_alignment.current_basis: Voluntary AI risk management for trustworthy AI systems, including design, development, use, and evaluation.
current_source_alignment.trust_pack_implication: Treat governance, measurement, lifecycle risk, and critical-infrastructure trust as procurement evidence gates.

current_source_alignment.current_basis: The core functions are govern, map, measure, and manage, with governance cross-cutting the lifecycle.
current_source_alignment.trust_pack_implication: Map the app to govern/map/measure/manage evidence instead of relying on generic responsible-AI copy.

current_source_alignment.current_basis: SSDF gives producers and purchasers a common secure-development vocabulary for acquisition conversations.
current_source_alignment.trust_pack_implication: Use repeatable tests, dependency scans, least-privilege RLS, and release evidence as buyer-facing proof.

current_source_alignment.current_basis: Secure by Design emphasizes ownership of customer security outcomes, transparency, accountability, and leadership commitment.
current_source_alignment.trust_pack_implication: Do not transfer verification burden to buyers; show explicit owner gates and redacted proof paths.

current_source_alignment.current_basis: Buyer-side procurement should ask software suppliers direct questions about secure-by-design practices.
current_source_alignment.trust_pack_implication: Provide a procurement questionnaire that separates current proof, missing proof, and allowed claims.

current_source_alignment.current_basis: The 2025 risks include prompt injection, sensitive information disclosure, supply chain, output handling, excessive agency, misinformation, and unbounded consumption.
current_source_alignment.trust_pack_implication: Add LLM threat questions before any enterprise or public-sector AI claim is upgraded.

current_source_alignment.current_basis: ISO/IEC 42001 defines requirements for an AI management system and is applicable across public and private sectors.
current_source_alignment.trust_pack_implication: Position this pack as pre-certification evidence mapping, not ISO certification or an audited AI management system.

procurement_questionnaire.buyer_question: Can this be represented as enterprise-ready or world-class accurate today?
procurement_questionnaire.current_answer: No. The current decision remains pilot-only and market language must stay buyer-safe.
procurement_questionnaire.missing_proof: Hosted proof, buyer validation, RLS test evidence, and real accuracy baselines.
procurement_questionnaire.owner_action: Approve pilot-only positioning and prohibit world-class prediction claims until evidence gates pass.
procurement_questionnaire.allowed_claim: Governed strategic-intelligence pilot with calibration-aware decision support.

procurement_questionnaire.buyer_question: Are tenant, user, reviewer, and public/private boundaries enforced and tested?
procurement_questionnaire.current_answer: A first-batch draft and test plan exist, but they are not applied or executed.
procurement_questionnaire.missing_proof: Owner classification, migration, pgTAP pass, linked Supabase verification, and hosted smoke.
procurement_questionnaire.owner_action: Review classifications, resolve open owner decisions, and approve one narrow RLS migration.
procurement_questionnaire.allowed_claim: RLS remediation path is drafted; enterprise isolation proof is not complete.

procurement_questionnaire.buyer_question: Which tables are intentionally public or anonymous, and why?
procurement_questionnaire.current_answer: The classification register identifies public/anonymous exception reviews, but owner decisions are pending.
procurement_questionnaire.missing_proof: Approved data category, privacy impact, buyer-safe rationale, and tests for each exception.
procurement_questionnaire.owner_action: Mark every public/anonymous exception as approved, removed, or narrowed.

procurement_questionnaire.buyer_question: Does the hosted app match the repo-backed launch surface?
procurement_questionnaire.current_answer: No hosted/live proof is attached; the proof kit defines checks to run after owner-approved access.
procurement_questionnaire.missing_proof: Hosted URL, deploy id, access preflight, auth/schema/function drift, screenshots, and redacted logs.
procurement_questionnaire.owner_action: Provide hosted URL, deployment state, secrets policy, and smoke-user policy, then run hosted proof kit.
procurement_questionnaire.allowed_claim: Hosted proof plan exists; hosted behavior is not proven in this artifact.

procurement_questionnaire.buyer_question: Can paid/premium access and account boundaries be demonstrated?
procurement_questionnaire.current_answer: Scripts exist for hosted diagnostics; no hosted entitlement proof is attached.
procurement_questionnaire.missing_proof: Hosted auth diagnostics, invalid webhook rejection proof, and entitlement table evidence.
procurement_questionnaire.owner_action: Run hosted auth/payment proof after owner-approved secrets and payment test policy.
procurement_questionnaire.allowed_claim: Auth and entitlement proof path is defined.

procurement_questionnaire.buyer_question: Has the app been tested against prompt injection and instruction override attacks?
procurement_questionnaire.current_answer: Static prompt-injection controls are inventoried and the local non-secret red-team fixtures passed; hosted model-path testing remains pending.
procurement_questionnaire.missing_proof: Hosted prompt-injection regression tests with redacted logs and buyer-visible evidence.
procurement_questionnaire.owner_action: Approve hosted fixture execution policy, redacted evidence paths, and smoke-user scope.
procurement_questionnaire.allowed_claim: Local prompt-injection regression coverage exists; hosted resistance is not proven.

procurement_questionnaire.buyer_question: Can the app prevent or flag sensitive data leakage through LLM output or logs?
procurement_questionnaire.current_answer: Static diagnostics/redaction controls are inventoried and local canary fixtures passed; hosted output/log redaction proof is pending.
procurement_questionnaire.missing_proof: Hosted sensitive-data fixtures, log-redaction inspection, and hosted output proof.
procurement_questionnaire.owner_action: Run hosted redaction and sensitive-output checks with non-secret fixtures after approval.
procurement_questionnaire.allowed_claim: Local sensitive-disclosure fixture coverage exists; hosted redaction proof is pending.

procurement_questionnaire.buyer_question: Are LLM outputs validated before downstream use?
procurement_questionnaire.current_answer: Static audit confirms structured schemas, sanitizers, evidence-reference filtering, provenance downgrade, and local adversarial fixture execution.
procurement_questionnaire.missing_proof: Hosted adversarial output fixtures for buyer-critical flows.
procurement_questionnaire.owner_action: Run hosted output-handling fixtures with redacted evidence.
procurement_questionnaire.allowed_claim: Local output-handling red-team fixtures passed; hosted model-path coverage is pending.

procurement_questionnaire.buyer_question: Can the AI take irreversible or high-impact actions automatically?
procurement_questionnaire.current_answer: The AI action inventory maps 10 action surfaces and 7 high-impact product actions. The draft high-impact action policy defines 10 policy surfaces, 9 approval-required surfaces, 9 hosted boundary tests, and 4 static boundary tests. It found 0 direct LLM-to-irreversible-action surfaces, but owner approval count is 0 and hosted verified test count is 0.
procurement_questionnaire.missing_proof: Owner-approved high-impact action policy, hosted no-autonomous-action test execution, RLS proof, payment/webhook invalid-signature proof, and redacted hosted evidence.
procurement_questionnaire.owner_action: Review the draft high-impact action policy, approve or edit every approval-required surface, then run hosted action-boundary smoke tests with redacted evidence.

procurement_questionnaire.current_answer: Calibration primitives, sample ledger mechanics, and a protocol exist; no real owner-approved resolved-outcome proof exists.
procurement_questionnaire.missing_proof: Owner-approved resolved forecast export, real comparable baselines, leakage review, and hosted calibration proof.
procurement_questionnaire.owner_action: Supply approved forecast/baseline data and run calibration plus benchmark scripts under protocol gates.

acceptance_gates.command_after_approval: convert approved draft into supabase/migrations plus supabase test db
PATH=$HOME/.nvm/versions/node/v20.19.5/bin:$PATH npm run diag:hosted:access && npm run smoke:strategist:hosted
Run owner-approved hosted LLM security smoke with non-secret fixtures and redacted logs.

Review the draft high-impact action policy, approve or edit every approval-required surface, then run hosted no-autonomous-action smoke for publication, review, payment/webhook, and post-analysis fanout boundaries.
run audit:calibration:ledger and audit:forecast:benchmark on owner-approved resolved data and baselines
acceptance_gates.unlocks: accuracy claim upgrade, if results support it

acceptance_gates.command_after_approval: attach data inventory, retention, deletion, subprocessors, DPA, support, and SLA terms
refresh npm audit, build, focused tests, and release checklist before external procurement review

recommended_external_sharing_gate: Owner reviews every questionnaire answer and marks rows approved for external sharing.
Remove internal script names, logs, or sensitive architecture details for public-sector procurement where needed.
Attach only redacted hosted smoke evidence and never include secrets.
Keep the prohibited claims list visible in any buyer-facing security packet.
